codeReview.summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff. The implementation covers the expected behavior: CLI/help wording is aligned to mors-native auth semantics, deploy tests now assert explicit flyctl remediation/category details, cli-encrypted-remote no-server paths avoid hardcoded-port collisions, onboard persistence now fails loudly when profile persistence regresses, multi-device secure-delivery continuity has explicit assertions, and duplicate-handle relay onboarding is covered with concurrent race verification including loser no-profile persistence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill scope text appears stale relative to mission feature assignment: this feature uses mors-global-worker but SKILL.md's 'When to Use' milestone list does not include misc-hardening milestones.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:1747-1749 assigns misc-hardening-3-native-auth-polish to skillName=mors-global-worker; .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:12 lists only relay-foundation/global-async-messaging/realtime-watch/developer-launch-path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Port-range guidance may be ambiguous for no-server negative-path tests: AGENTS constrains ports to 3100-3109 for new services, while this stabilization uses randomized high fake relay ports to avoid collisions. Clarifying that non-bound fake URLs are exempt would reduce reviewer/worker ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mission AGENTS.md:7 says use local ports 3100-3109 for new services; test/relay/cli-encrypted-remote.test.ts:405-408 uses noServerPort=39_999+random and fakeRelayUrl for no-server prerequisite tests.

summary: Pass. The reviewed changes and added assertions align with the feature’s hardening goals, and no blocking defects were found in the implemented scope.
